decks.notes: The main idea of this deck is to create a powerful card advantage engine using Paradoxical Outcome with artifacts like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith to generate mana quickly and draw cards. The key win condition is to assemble a combo using Coveted Jewel and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain to generate a large amount of mana and control the game with cards like Force of Will and Chalice of the Void. The deck also includes powerful backup options like Karn, the Great Creator and Tinker to search for key artifacts like Time Vault for additional win conditions.

This deck focuses on utilizing the power of Paradoxical Outcome to generate massive card advantage and mana while controlling the opponent's board. It includes powerful restricted cards like Black Lotus, Ancestral Recall, and Time Walk to accelerate its game plan. The deck also runs key artifact mana like Moxen and Mana Crypt to ensure fast mana development. With strong combo potential and the ability to disrupt opponents with counterspells and removal, this deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful card draw and mana generation.

The main strategy of this Combo-Dredge deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Prized Amalgam, Narcomoeba, and Stinkweed Imp, while using cards like Shambling Shell and Golgari Thug to dredge through the deck and bring creatures back from the graveyard. The deck also includes disruptive spells like Force of Negation and Force of Will to protect the combo pieces, as well as cards like Serum Powder and Bazaar of Baghdad to help with card selection and graveyard filling. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and consistent graveyard-based strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing the combo potential of Underworld Breach along with various powerful cards such as Ancestral Recall, Black Lotus, and Paradoxical Outcome to create explosive plays and generate value. With the support of cards like Lurrus of the Dream-Den and Stock Up, the deck aims to consistently draw into its combo pieces and disrupt opponent's strategies using counterspells like Force of Will. The synergy between cards like Artist's Talent and Flame of Anor adds an extra layer of versatility to the deck, making it a potent threat in a Vintage 1 vs 1 environment.

This deck focuses on utilizing control elements, such as counterspells and removal, to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage and resource management. It also includes versatile creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Lavinia, Azorius Renegade to further control the board. The inclusion of powerful cards like Black Lotus and Ancestral Recall allows for explosive plays, while cards like Time Walk and Lurrus of the Dream-Den provide additional value and flexibility. Overall, the deck aims to outmaneuver the opponent and steadily secure victory through strategic decision-making and efficient use of resources.

This deck focuses on using aggressive creatures, efficient spells, and disruptive elements to quickly overwhelm the opponent and maintain control over the game. It utilizes a mix of card advantage, graveyard recursion, and tempo plays to stay ahead in the match. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value and a resilient threat that can help close out games. The deck aims to apply pressure early and disrupt the opponent's game plan, ultimately leading to a swift victory.

The deck focuses on aggressive creatures like Psychic Frog and Orcish Bowmasters, backed up by disruption spells like Force of Will and Fatal Push. It also includes powerful cards like Force of Negation, Ancestral Recall, and Time Walk to gain advantage. The deck aims to control the board while applying pressure with efficient creatures and disrupting the opponent's game plan. Lurrus of the Dream-Den gives the deck additional value by allowing the player to recur low-cost permanents. Overall, the deck is designed to be proactive and disruptive to gain an edge over the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play, while also having access to control elements like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect its game plan. It has a strong suite of mana acceleration with cards like Black Lotus and Mana Crypt, as well as card selection tools like Ponder and Brainstorm. Additionally, the deck utilizes powerful spells like Time Walk and Demonic Tutor to further its strategy of deploying threats and disrupting the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Paradoxical Outcome card to generate a large amount of card advantage and mana by bouncing back key artifacts to hand. By combining fast mana sources like Black Lotus and Moxen with card draw and countermagic, the deck aims to quickly assemble a winning combo or overwhelming board presence. Lurrus of the Dream-Den provides additional value by allowing the player to recast key cards from the graveyard. The overall strategy revolves around efficiently cycling through the deck while setting up explosive turns to overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on using artifact creatures and powerful artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent and disrupt their strategy. Cards like Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb allow for fast mana acceleration to cast threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Scrawling Crawler. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere to slow down the opponent, while cards like Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional threats and utility. The goal is to apply constant pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board state and resources using counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce, along with removal spells like Fatal Push. It also aims to disrupt the opponent's strategies with cards like Null Rod and Wasteland. The deck also utilizes card draw and card selection spells like Ancestral Recall, Ponder, and Brainstorm to maintain card advantage and find key pieces. Lurrus of the Dream-Den serves as a companion, providing additional value by allowing the player to cast certain cards from the graveyard.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to maintain control over the game while utilizing powerful cards and disruptive spells to outmaneuver opponents. With a strong suite of counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation, along with removal spells like Fatal Push, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect its own threats. The inclusion of powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time allows the deck to consistently find answers and maintain card advantage. Overall, the deck seeks to control the flow of the game and outvalue opponents with efficient spells and powerful threats.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on using powerful counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Psychic Frog to control the game. It also utilizes graveyard hate with Nihil Spellbomb and card advantage with Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and card draw spells like Ancestral Recall, Gush, and Treasure Cruise. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan, control the board, and eventually win the game through efficient use of its resources and controlling the flow of the game.

The deck focuses on generating card advantage and mana efficiency through Paradoxical Outcome, allowing the player to continuously bounce and replay their artifacts for maximum value. The deck also includes control elements such as Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion adds versatility and resilience to the strategy, making it a well-rounded and powerful combo deck in Vintage 1 vs 1 format.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's plays while generating card advantage and maintaining board presence. The deck utilizes powerful control spells such as Force of Will, Force of Negation, and Fatal Push to deal with threats, while also utilizing card draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Ponder to keep up with opponent's card advantage. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Underground Sea and Wasteland to disrupt opponent's mana base and slow down their development. This deck aims to out-value the opponent and win the game through attrition.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to cast Doomsday and win the game by using Thassa's Oracle to manipulate the top of the library. The deck aims to control the game with counterspells and disruptive spells while digging through the deck with cantrips and card draw to find the combo pieces. Lurrus of the Dream-Den is included as a companion to provide additional value and recursion for the deck. The deck can win the game quickly and efficiently with the right combination of cards and strategic gameplay.
